summary: The 370Z is a fundamentally reliable sports car. The VQ37VHR engine is strong and durable, and the chassis is simple. Main concerns are the oil gallery gasket and CSC on manual cars.

issues.name: Oil Gallery Gasket Leak
issues.description: The oil gallery gasket between the engine block and oil cooler can fail, causing a significant oil leak. Can lead to oil starvation if not addressed.

issues.name: Concentric Slave Cylinder (CSC) Failure
issues.description: The manual transmission's concentric slave cylinder is located inside the bellhousing and can fail, requiring transmission removal to replace.

issues.name: Timing Chain Guide Wear
issues.description: The VQ37 timing chain guides can wear over time, causing a rattling noise on cold start. Rarely catastrophic but should be monitored.

issues.name: Clutch Pedal Position Sensor Issues
issues.description: The clutch pedal position sensor can fail, causing the SynchroRev Match system to malfunction or the car to not start.

issues.name: Window Regulator Failure
issues.description: The power window regulators can fail, causing the windows to stop working or drop into the door panel.

buyingTips: Check underneath for oil gallery gasket leaks — look for oil dripping from the driver's side of the engine near the oil filter
Test the clutch feel on manual cars — the CSC is a known weakness. A soft or spongy pedal may indicate CSC wear
The VQ37VHR is best maintained with 5W-30 synthetic oil changed every 5,000 miles — verify service history
The 370Z was sold from 2009–2020 with minimal changes — later models have minor updates but are fundamentally identical
Check for accident history — the 370Z is affordable and commonly driven hard by young enthusiasts
